description: Lightweight routing manifest for the public Agentic Knowledge Vault demo.

folders.00_Raw: Original source notes and public-safe assets.
folders.00_Raw/Inbox/Class Notes: Preserved class discussion note used in this public demo.
folders.00_Raw/Sources: Stable external source captures, organized by source type.
folders.00_Raw/Sources/YouTube: YouTube transcripts and video source captures.
folders.00_Raw/Assets: Public-safe attachments (images, PDFs, screenshots) when needed.
folders.01_Wiki/Projects: Project notes, coursework synthesis, and information-story examples.
folders.01_Wiki/Projects/Class Notes: Processed course notes and assignment materials.
folders.01_Wiki/Source_Notes: Processed summaries and routing notes for specific sources.
folders.02_Meta: Lightweight indexes, manifest, schema, templates, and maintenance log.

notes.title: Portable Information Structures - Weeks 1-5 Class Synthesis
notes.summary: Weeks 1-5 frame portable information structures as human-and-machine-readable structures that preserve meaning, access, interoperability, and reuse across contexts while managing privacy and security.

notes.title: Portable Information Structures
notes.summary: Portable information structures are documented structures that preserve meaning, access, and reuse as information moves across contexts and systems.
related: Data Portability vs Data Usability
Information Stories
FAIR Principles for Agentic Knowledge Bases
Portable Information Structures - Weeks 1-5 Class Synthesis

notes.title: Data Portability vs Data Usability
notes.summary: Data can be portable without being usable when meaning, context, tools, size, access paths, or user needs are not preserved.
related: Portable Information Structures
Information Stories
FAIR Principles for Agentic Knowledge Bases

notes.title: Information Stories
notes.summary: Information stories adapt user stories to specify who needs what information, from what structure, and for what goal.
related: Portable Information Structures
LLM Wiki as a Portable Information Structure
Data Portability vs Data Usability

notes.title: FAIR Principles for Agentic Knowledge Bases
notes.summary: FAIR principles can be adapted for agentic knowledge bases by making notes findable, accessible, interoperable, and reusable for both humans and AI agents.
related: Portable Information Structures
LLM Wiki as a Portable Information Structure
Data Portability vs Data Usability
HTML as an Agent Output Format

notes.path: 01_Wiki/Questions/How should an LLM wiki apply FAIR principles.md
notes.title: How should an LLM wiki apply FAIR principles
notes.summary: An LLM wiki applies FAIR by making raw sources and processed notes findable, accessible, interoperable, and reusable through paths, metadata, links, and provenance.
related: LLM Wiki as a Portable Information Structure
FAIR Principles for Agentic Knowledge Bases
Portable Information Structures

notes.title: Prompting 101 - Code with Claude
notes.summary: Anthropic's Applied AI team walks through prompt-engineering best practices by iteratively building a single prompt for a Swedish car-insurance claim task, demonstrating a ten-part prompt structure.
related: Prompt Engineering Structure
HTML as an Agent Output Format

notes.title: Thariq - The Unreasonable Effectiveness of HTML
notes.summary: Thariq argues for preferring HTML over Markdown as the output format when working with Claude Code, because HTML is denser, easier to read and share, interactive, and keeps the human in the loop.
source_paths: 00_Raw/Sources/Web_Posts/Thariq - Using Claude Code - The Unreasonable Effectiveness of HTML.md
related: HTML as an Agent Output Format

Data Portability vs Data Usability

notes.title: Prompt Engineering Structure
notes.summary: A layered prompt structure - role, context, instructions, examples, reminders, and output controls - that makes one-shot LLM tasks more reliable.
related: Prompting 101 - Code with Claude
HTML as an Agent Output Format
FAIR Principles for Agentic Knowledge Bases

notes.title: HTML as an Agent Output Format
notes.summary: HTML is often a better artifact format than Markdown for agent output because it is denser, more readable, shareable, and interactive - at the cost of tokens, speed, and diffability.
